sizing_rule.note: Van Tharp 1R method: divide max acceptable risk per trade (1.5% of account) by stop-loss distance in dollars to determine position size. Enforce constraint that cumulative positions do not exceed available buying power or margin capacity. Max 3 concurrent positions, each capped at 40% of account value.

source_rule_text: A trader should calculate position size by dividing maximum acceptable risk per trade (as a dollar amount) by the stop-loss distance, and verify that cumulative position sizes do not exceed the account's actual buying power or margin capacity.
